title: Odyssey, Book 21: The Bow
description: Book 21 of Homer's Odyssey: The Bow.
introduction: Book 21 of the Odyssey: The Bow. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.original_text: Τῇ δ᾿ ἄρ᾿ ἐπὶ φρεσὶ θῆκε θεὰ γλαυκῶπις Ἀθήνη.
translation.text: Athena put it in Penelope's mind to set the contest of the bow. Whoever could string Odysseus' great bow and shoot an arrow through twelve axe heads would win her hand. One by one the suitors tried and failed. Odysseus asked to try. Despite their protests, he took the bow, strung it easily, and shot the arrow through all twelve axes.
